extra.message: User input from request.args.get('name') (app/blueprints/sandboxes/views.py:13) is rendered with the Jinja2 |safe filter, which disables HTML escaping. This allows reflected XSS via the 'name' GET parameter.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation (Cross-site Scripting)

extra.message: User input from request.cookies.get('name') or request.form.get('name') (app/blueprints/sandboxes/views.py:27-28) is rendered with the Jinja2 |safe filter, which disables HTML escaping. This allows stored/reflected XSS via the 'name' cookie or POST parameter.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation (Cross-site Scripting)

extra.message: The 'message' field from the database is rendered with the Jinja2 |safe filter. Messages can be created without authentication via the API (app/blueprints/api/views.py:43-56) or by any logged-in user via /account_area/send_feedback (app/blueprints/account_area/views.py:63-78). This allows unauthenticated stored XSS attacks.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation (Cross-site Scripting)

extra.message: The 'from_user' field from the database is rendered with the Jinja2 |safe filter. This value can be set to arbitrary content via the unauthenticated API POST endpoint (app/blueprints/api/views.py:50). This allows stored XSS attacks.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation (Cross-site Scripting)

extra.message: The non-admin user's username (g.user.username) is rendered with the Jinja2 |safe filter in the base template, which is inherited by all pages. If an admin creates or edits a user with script tags in the username via Flask-Admin, the XSS payload will execute on every page visited by that user.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation (Cross-site Scripting)

extra.message: User input from request.args.get('next') is passed directly to flask.redirect() without any URL validation. An attacker can craft a URL like /toggle_xss_protection/?next=https://evil.com to redirect users to arbitrary external sites.

cwe: CWE-601: URL Redirection to Untrusted Site (Open Redirect)

extra.message: The Flask SECRET_KEY is hardcoded as a plaintext string 'Keep it secret, keep it safe' in the source code. This key is used to sign session cookies and CSRF tokens. Anyone with access to the source code can forge valid session cookies and impersonate any user.
DEBUG mode is hardcoded to True. In production, this would expose detailed stack traces and the Werkzeug interactive debugger, which allows remote code execution via the debugger console.

User passwords are stored in plaintext (db.Column(db.String(200))) and compared directly without hashing (app/blueprints/login/views.py:17). The serialize property (line 27) also exposes plaintext passwords via the API. This means a database breach exposes all user credentials in cleartext.

cwe: CWE-256: Plaintext Storage of a Password

extra.message: The UsersView.index() API endpoint returns all users with their passwords via the .serialize property (app/models/models.py:27). While protected by @admin_required, plaintext passwords should never be included in API responses.

cwe: CWE-200: Exposure of Sensitive Information to an Unauthorized Actor

extra.message: MessagesView.post() has no authentication requirement (no @admin_required decorator). Unlike all other MessagesView methods (index, get, delete, acknowledge_message) which require admin access via @admin_required, the post method allows unauthenticated attackers to create messages with arbitrary content, including XSS payloads that will be rendered unsanitized in the admin panel.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: The change_password endpoint accepts POST requests without any CSRF token validation. Combined with the lack of CSRF protection across all forms (login, change_email, send_feedback, unsafe_cookies), an attacker can forge requests to change a victim's password, email, or submit feedback on their behalf.
The add_admin management command has a hardcoded default password 'admin'. If invoked without specifying a password, an admin account is created with credentials admin/admin.
